 {"id":"bd-1rz0.4.1","title":"Atomic Present Protocol + Shrink Cleanup","description":"## Task
Define the atomic present protocol and shrink cleanup strategy for the render pipeline.

## Scope
- Explicit sequence for present: clear, draw, sync, restore.
- Shrink cleanup rules to prevent ghosting.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for cleanup order and state reset.
- Snapshot tests during shrink/grow sequences.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Protocol is documented and enforceable.
- No residual artifacts after shrink.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.068647193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:18:08.098621797Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:15.800696304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:15.991178567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.068647193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.4.2","title":"Adaptive Buffer Allocation + Reuse Strategy","description":"Minimize allocations under resize by adaptive buffers and pool reuse.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Adaptive Buffer Allocation + Reuse Strategy
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema
- Correctness: add property tests for diff/present invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, no-ghosting on shrink)
- Golden outputs: checksum render outputs for canonical fixtures; ensure isomorphism under optimization
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.251111834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:10:21.105056812Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.251111834Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.4.3","title":"Inline Mode Reflow Policy (Auto vs Fixed)","description":"Define inline reflow behavior (auto UI height vs fixed) and migration path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Inline Mode Reflow Policy (Auto vs Fixed)
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Correctness: add property tests for diff/present invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, no-ghosting on shrink)
- Golden outputs: checksum render outputs for canonical fixtures; ensure isomorphism under optimization
- Layout invariants: feasibility, stable tie-breaking, monotone constraint response; add metamorphic/property tests

-{"id":"bd-1rz0.4.4","title":"Double-Buffer Swap: O(1) Buffer Transition","description":"## Goal
Replace buffer cloning with O(1) pointer swap for prev/current buffer transition.

## Background: Current Implementation
In ftui-runtime/src/terminal_writer.rs, after each frame:
```rust
// Current: O(n) clone where n = width * height * 16 bytes
self.prev_buffer = self.current_buffer.clone();
```

For a 120x40 terminal, this is 4,800 cells × 16 bytes = 76.8 KB copied every frame. At 60 FPS, that's 4.6 MB/s of unnecessary copying.

## Solution: Double-Buffer Swap
```rust
pub struct DoubleBuffer {
    buffers: [Buffer; 2],
    current_idx: usize,
}

impl DoubleBuffer {
    /// O(1) swap - just flip the index
    pub fn swap(&mut self) {
        self.current_idx = 1 - self.current_idx;
    }
    
    pub fn current(&self) -> &Buffer {
        &self.buffers[self.current_idx]
    }
    
    pub fn current_mut(&mut self) -> &mut Buffer {
        &mut self.buffers[self.current_idx]
    }
    
    pub fn previous(&self) -> &Buffer {
        &self.buffers[1 - self.current_idx]
    }
}
```

## Implementation Details

### Buffer Lifecycle
1. **Init**: Create two buffers of same dimensions
2. **Render**: Write to current_mut()
3. **Diff**: Compare current() vs previous()
4. **Present**: Emit ANSI for diff
5. **Swap**: Flip index (O(1))
6. **Clear**: Clear the NEW current buffer for next frame

### Clear Strategy
After swap, the 'current' buffer contains the OLD frame. We need to clear it before rendering the new frame. Options:
1. **Full clear**: Zero all cells (O(n) but cache-friendly)
2. **Lazy clear**: Mark as dirty, clear on write (complex)
3. **Diff-based clear**: Only clear cells that changed last frame (track dirty regions)

Recommendation: Full clear is simple and fast enough. With SIMD memset, clearing 76.8 KB takes ~10μs.

### Resize Handling
On resize, both buffers must be reallocated:
```rust
impl DoubleBuffer {
    pub fn resize(&mut self, new_width: u16, new_height: u16) {
        for buffer in &mut self.buffers {
            *buffer = Buffer::new(new_width, new_height);
        }
    }
}
```

## Invariants
1. Both buffers always have identical dimensions
2. current_idx is always 0 or 1
3. After swap, previous() returns what was current() before
4. Buffer contents are never shared (no aliasing)

## Performance Impact
- **Before**: O(n) clone per frame (~70μs for 120x40)
- **After**: O(1) swap (~1ns)
- **Net savings**: 70μs/frame = 4.2ms/s at 60 FPS

## Testing
- Unit tests for swap semantics
- Property tests: previous() after swap == current() before
- Benchmark: measure actual speedup
- Stress test: rapid resize + render cycles

## Files to Modify
- crates/ftui-render/src/buffer.rs (add DoubleBuffer)
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/terminal_writer.rs (use DoubleBuffer)
- crates/ftui-render/src/diff.rs (adjust if needed)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:26:45.704669120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:26:58.742992890Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","performance","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:26:45.704669120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":223,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why This Matters for Zero-Flicker Reflow

During a resize storm, frames are rendered rapidly. Each frame currently incurs a buffer clone. With double-buffer swap:

1. **Latency Reduction**: 70μs saved per frame = tighter render loop
2. **Memory Bandwidth**: Less pressure on memory bus during resize
3. **Cache Efficiency**: No unnecessary copying means more cache available for actual rendering
4. **Determinism**: Swap is O(1) regardless of buffer size, making timing predictable

### Relationship to Other Beads
- **bd-1rz0.4.2 (Adaptive Buffer Allocation)**: DoubleBuffer is the allocation strategy
- **bd-4kq0.1 (Dirty-Rect Diff)**: Diff benefits from stable buffer addresses
- **bd-1rz0.3 (Resize Coalescer)**: Faster frames = more responsive coalescing

### Implementation Notes
The swap pattern is standard in graphics (GPU swap chains, SDL double buffering). It's battle-tested and well-understood.

Key insight: We don't need to preserve buffer contents across resize. The atomic reflow (bd-1rz0.4) ensures a full re-render on resize, so both buffers can be reallocated fresh.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:26:58Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-1rz0.4.4","title":"Double-Buffer Swap: O(1) Buffer Transition","description":"## Goal
Replace buffer cloning with O(1) pointer swap for prev/current buffer transition.

## Background: Current Implementation
In ftui-runtime/src/terminal_writer.rs, after each frame:
```rust
// Current: O(n) clone where n = width * height * 16 bytes

```

For a 120x40 terminal, this is 4,800 cells × 16 bytes = 76.8 KB copied every frame. At 60 FPS, that's 4.6 MB/s of unnecessary copying.

## Solution: Double-Buffer Swap
```rust
pub struct DoubleBuffer {
    buffers: [Buffer; 2],
    current_idx: usize,
}

impl DoubleBuffer {

        self.current_idx = 1 - self.current_idx;

    pub fn current(&self) -> &Buffer {
        &self.buffers[self.current_idx]
    }
    
    pub fn current_mut(&mut self) -> &mut Buffer {
        &mut self.buffers[self.current_idx]
    }
    
    pub fn previous(&self) -> &Buffer {
        &self.buffers[1 - self.current_idx]
    }
}
```

## Implementation Details

### Buffer Lifecycle
1. **Init**: Create two buffers of same dimensions
2. **Render**: Write to current_mut()
3. **Diff**: Compare current() vs previous()
4. **Present**: Emit ANSI for diff
5. **Swap**: Flip index (O(1))
6. **Clear**: Clear the NEW current buffer for next frame

### Clear Strategy
After swap, the 'current' buffer contains the OLD frame. We need to clear it before rendering the new frame. Options:
1. **Full clear**: Zero all cells (O(n) but cache-friendly)
2. **Lazy clear**: Mark as dirty, clear on write (complex)
3. **Diff-based clear**: Only clear cells that changed last frame (track dirty regions)

Recommendation: Full clear is simple and fast enough. With SIMD memset, clearing 76.8 KB takes ~10μs.

### Resize Handling
On resize, both buffers must be reallocated:
```rust
impl DoubleBuffer {
    pub fn resize(&mut self, new_width: u16, new_height: u16) {
        for buffer in &mut self.buffers {
            *buffer = Buffer::new(new_width, new_height);
        }
    }
}
```

## Invariants
1. Both buffers always have identical dimensions
2. current_idx is always 0 or 1
3. After swap, previous() returns what was current() before
4. Buffer contents are never shared (no aliasing)

## Performance Impact
- **Before**: O(n) clone per frame (~70μs for 120x40)
- **After**: O(1) swap (~1ns)
- **Net savings**: 70μs/frame = 4.2ms/s at 60 FPS

## Testing
- Unit tests for swap semantics
- Property tests: previous() after swap == current() before
- Benchmark: measure actual speedup
- Stress test: rapid resize + render cycles

## Files to Modify
- crates/ftui-render/src/buffer.rs (add DoubleBuffer)
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/terminal_writer.rs (use DoubleBuffer)
- crates/ftui-render/src/diff.rs (adjust if needed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TerminalWriter no longer clones buffers per frame; uses an O(1) swap.
- [ ] Unit/property tests validate swap semantics and resize behavior.
- [ ] Benchmarks show measurable reduction in per-frame time and/or bandwidth on representative sizes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:26:45.704669120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:29:32.598487364Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","performance","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:26:45.704669120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":223,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why This Matters for Zero-Flicker Reflow

During a resize storm, frames are rendered rapidly. Each frame currently incurs a buffer clone. With double-buffer swap:

1. **Latency Reduction**: 70μs saved per frame = tighter render loop

- **bd-1rz0.3 (Resize Coalescer)**: Faster frames = more responsive coalescing

The swap pattern is standard in graphics (GPU swap chains, SDL double buffering). It's battle-tested and well-understood.

Key insight: We don't need to preserve buffer contents across resize. The atomic reflow (bd-1rz0.4) ensures a full re-render on resize, so both buffers can be reallocated fresh.

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.5","title":"Responsive Layout Primitives (Breakpoints + Auto UI Height)","description":"## Task
Add responsive layout primitives: breakpoints and optional auto UI height for inline mode.

## Scope
- Breakpoint model (xs/sm/md/lg) with helper selectors.
- Auto UI height measurement pass with caching.
- Ensure inline mode respects measured height.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for breakpoint matching and selector logic.
- Snapshot tests at multiple terminal sizes.
- PTY E2E: resize across breakpoints with JSONL logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Layout switches predictably at breakpoints.
- Auto height does not thrash or flicker.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:23.413089957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:17:41.528212809Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:23.413089957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:25.518152066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.5.1","title":"Responsive Layout: Breakpoint System","description":"## Task
Define breakpoint model and responsive selector helpers for layout variants.

## Scope
- Breakpoint enum + size thresholds.
- Helper API for conditional layout selection.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for threshold boundaries.
- Snapshot tests at each breakpoint size.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Breakpoint selection is deterministic and documented.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.612993647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:17:49.552104527Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:16.446674667Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.612993647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.5.2","title":"Auto UI Height Measurement Pass","description":"## Task
Implement auto UI height measurement pass with caching to avoid repeated cost.

## Scope
- Measure UI content height once per stable layout.
- Cache result and invalidate on layout changes.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: cache hit/miss behavior.
- PTY E2E: resize + content changes with JSONL logs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Auto height is stable and does not flicker.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.801083132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:17:58.547366711Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:16.627356049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.801083132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -770,7 +770,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","title":"PlasmaFx: Theme-Derived Palettes + Presets","description":"## Task
Provide theme-derived palettes and plasma presets tuned for legibility.

## Scope
- Palette generation from `ResolvedTheme` with fallback defaults.
- Presets: Subtle, Neon, Monochrome.

## Tests / Validation
- Unit tests: palette stop counts and bounds.
- Snapshot tests: presets produce distinct visuals with readable overlay text.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Palettes are deterministic and theme-aware.
- Default preset is safe for all themes.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:24.817434914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:16.451163090Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["theme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:41:58.234835733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:24.817434914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":182,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Palette strategy:
- Use 2-4 accent colors from ThemeInputs.
- Provide interpolation function (lerp) and multi-stop sampling.
- Keep it local to visual-fx; do not depend on text-effects APIs.

Presets:
- AuroraPlasma (cool)
- EmberPlasma (warm)
- NeonPlasma (high saturation)

Acceptance:
- [ ] Palettes look good across themes.
- [ ] Palette switching does not require allocation.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.4.3","title":"PlasmaFx: Determinism + Performance/Quality Tests","description":"## Task
Add determinism and performance/quality tests for PlasmaFx.

## Tests
- Determinism: fixed seed/time yields identical output.
- Quality: lower quality reduces sample count and render time.
- Snapshot: golden frames for preset + quality combinations.
- Allocation: no per-frame allocations after warm-up.

## Logging
- JSONL logs include seed, quality, palette ID, and timings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests cover determinism, performance, and quality behavior.
- Snapshot diffs are stable across platforms.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:35.260426176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:26.606820101Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:35.260426176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Tests:
- spatial variance: sample multiple points and ensure not all identical
- determinism: same inputs -> same output hash
- quality honoring: Full vs Reduced produces different output but same dimensions

Acceptance:
- [ ] tests are robust and do not depend on demo code
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5","title":"Demo Integration (Phased): Reuse Buffers + Adopt Extracted FX","description":"Refactor `ftui-demo-showcase` to (1) eliminate per-frame allocations in VisualEffectsScreen, (2) adopt extracted `ftui-extras` FX for metaballs/plasma, and (3) optionally keep hi-res rendering via canvas adapters.

This bead is intentionally phased to unblock perf work early:
- `bd-l8x9.5.1`: Painter/Canvas reuse (no per-frame allocations)
- `bd-l8x9.5.2`: metaballs/plasma via extracted BackdropFx (cell-space)
- `bd-l8x9.5.3` (optional): regain hi-res via canvas adapters
- `bd-l8x9.5.4`: fix CLI help text numbering so humans + scripts agree

## Acceptance Criteria
- VisualEffectsScreen is allocation-free in steady state.
- Metaballs/plasma paths in the demo are driven by `ftui-extras` (no duplicated algorithms).
- No behavior regressions in input handling or transitions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:47.128608152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:28:50.704445082Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","polish","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:35:47.128608152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":184,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Current Problem

`VisualEffectsScreen::view()` currently does:
- `let mut painter = Painter::for_area(...)` each frame

This allocates (pixels + colors vectors) every frame and undermines "insanely high performance".

## Refactor Goals

- Metaballs and Plasma should call the extracted `ftui-extras` code (no duplicated algorithms).
- Avoid per-frame Painter allocation by storing painter(s) inside screen state and resizing only on terminal resize.
- Keep existing transitions/controls.

## Implementation Notes

Approach A (preferred):
- Extract effect *core* so it can render both:
  - cell-space (BackdropFx)
  - painter-space (Canvas adapter)
- Demo uses painter-space to keep the wow factor.

Approach B (acceptable interim):
- Use Backdrop-only rendering for metaballs/plasma in the demo (lower-res but still good), while other effects keep using painter.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No per-frame Painter allocations in steady state.
- [ ] Metaballs and Plasma demo visuals are driven by library code.
- [ ] No behavior regressions in input/transition handling.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:36:14Z"},{"id":202,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Plan Update: Split Into Phases (Unblock Perf Work)

This issue was previously blocked on `bd-l8x9.3`/`.4` (extracted effects) and `bd-l8x9.6` (canvas adapters).

That made the demo perf fix unnecessarily wait on library extraction.

New structure:
- Phase 1: stop per-frame allocations in `VisualEffectsScreen` (Painter reuse + resize-only growth)
- Phase 2: switch metaballs/plasma to the extracted `ftui-extras` BackdropFx implementation using **cell-space** rendering (lowest coupling)
- Phase 3 (optional): switch metaballs/plasma back to **hi-res** via canvas adapters (keeping the wow factor) once adapters exist

Each phase is tracked as its own subtask with the correct blocking dependencies.","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:08Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","title":"Demo Perf: Reuse Painter Buffers (No Per-Frame Allocations)","description":"## Task
Refactor VisualEffectsScreen to reuse Painter/Canvas buffers across frames (grow-only), eliminating steady-state allocations.

## Scope
- Cache painter buffers in screen state.
- Resize-only growth; clear on size changes.
- Keep per-effect data isolated from shared buffers.

## Tests / Validation
- Allocation test: render 1000 frames and assert no allocations after warm-up.
- Unit test: resize triggers buffer growth and clears appropriately.
- Perf log: record per-frame time before/after and include in JSONL.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zero allocations after warm-up at stable terminal size.
- No visual regressions vs baseline snapshots.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:18.387194507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:36.332215704Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","performance","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:18.387194507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Notes

Target file:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs`

Current anti-pattern:
- `Painter::for_area(...)` per frame (allocates `pixels` + `colors` vectors every frame).

Refactor sketch:
- Store one or more `Painter` instances inside `VisualEffectsScreen` state.
- Track the last required subpixel dimensions and `Mode`.
- On render:
  - compute required subpixel dims from `area` and `Mode`
  - if dims unchanged: `painter.clear()` and reuse
  - if dims grew: reallocate once, then reuse
  - if dims shrank: keep capacity; just treat as smaller logical size

Validation:
- Run `./scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` (screen navigation exercises the visual effects screen).
- Add a focused no-alloc check (unit test or debug-only counter) that asserts no `Vec` growth occurs after the first render at a stable size.

Acceptance:
- [ ] steady-state frames in VisualEffectsScreen allocate 0 bytes
- [ ] resize triggers at most one reallocation per growth event
- [ ] no behavior regressions (transitions/controls still work)","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:50Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","title":"Demo Perf: Reuse Painter Buffers (No Per-Frame Allocations)","description":"## Goal
Refactor VisualEffectsScreen to reuse `Painter`/`Canvas` buffers across frames (grow-only), eliminating steady-state allocations.

## Scope
- Cache painter buffers in screen state.
- Resize-only growth; clear on size changes.
- Keep per-effect state isolated from shared buffers.

## Tests / Validation
- Proxy allocation test: stable terminal size does not grow painter buffer capacity after warm-up.
- Unit test: resize triggers growth and clears appropriately.
- E2E: `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` should exercise the VFX screen once screen 12 is included.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No per-frame buffer growth after warm-up at a stable terminal size.
- No behavior regressions in VFX rendering/transitions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:18.387194507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:29:13.448492917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","performance","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:18.387194507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Notes

Target file:
- `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs`

Current anti-pattern:
- `Painter::for_area(...)` per frame (allocates `pixels` + `colors` vectors every frame).

Refactor sketch:
- Store one or more `Painter` instances inside `VisualEffectsScreen` state.
- Track the last required subpixel dimensions and `Mode`.
- On render:
  - compute required subpixel dims from `area` and `Mode`
  - if dims unchanged: `painter.clear()` and reuse
  - if dims grew: reallocate once, then reuse
  - if dims shrank: keep capacity; just treat as smaller logical size

Validation:
- Run `./scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` (screen navigation exercises the visual effects screen).
- Add a focused no-alloc check (unit test or debug-only counter) that asserts no `Vec` growth occurs after the first render at a stable size.

Acceptance:
- [ ] steady-state frames in VisualEffectsScreen allocate 0 bytes
- [ ] resize triggers at most one reallocation per growth event
- [ ] no behavior regressions (transitions/controls still work)","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:57:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","title":"Demo Integration Phase 2: Use Extracted Metaballs/Plasma Backdrops (Cell-Space)","description":"## Goal
Switch VisualEffectsScreen metaballs/plasma to call the extracted `ftui-extras` FX code via the Backdrop widget (cell-space rendering).

## Why Cell-Space First
This phase validates composability + code reuse with the lowest coupling.
Hi-res rendering can return later via canvas adapters (bd-l8x9.6 + bd-l8x9.5.3).

## Scope
- Replace demo-owned metaballs/plasma algorithm code with calls into `ftui-extras`.
- Render backdrop first, then render existing overlays (help/FPS) above.

## Tests / Validation
- Covered by `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` once screen 12 is included (bd-l8x9.8.1).
- Add a focused regression check: starting the VFX screen must not panic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Metaballs/plasma demo output is driven by `ftui-extras` (no local copies).
- No behavior regressions in input/transition handling.
- Plays nicely with scrim/legibility defaults when overlays are present.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:06.189936554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:28:58.870020252Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:06.189936554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:06.189936554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:06.189936554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:06.189936554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":204,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Notes

This phase intentionally prioritizes **code reuse + composability validation** over maximum visual fidelity.

Why:
- It proves the extracted `BackdropFx` + `Backdrop` widget can drive a real screen.
- It eliminates duplicated metaballs/plasma logic inside `visual_effects.rs`.
- It gives us a working baseline while `bd-l8x9.6` (canvas adapters) remains optional.

Implementation outline:
- In `VisualEffectsScreen::view()`:
  - for `EffectType::Metaballs` and `EffectType::Plasma`, render a `Backdrop<MetaballsFx/PlasmaFx>` into the screen area.
  - render any existing help/FPS overlay text after the backdrop so it remains readable.
- Keep the rest of the effects unchanged (they can continue using the existing canvas painter).

Acceptance:
- [ ] metaballs/plasma visual output is driven by `ftui-extras` (no local algorithm copies)
- [ ] screen transitions and keyboard controls unchanged
- [ ] no per-frame allocations (relies on Phase 1)","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","title":"Demo Integration Phase 3 (Optional): Hi-Res Metaballs/Plasma via Canvas Adapters","description":"## Goal (Optional)
Restore the showcase "wow" for metaballs/plasma by rendering them at sub-cell resolution via canvas adapters while still using the same extracted core algorithms.

## Scope
- Use the canvas adapters from `bd-l8x9.6` to render metaballs/plasma into a reused `Painter`.
- Convert to `Canvas` and render.
- Keep the Painter reuse work from `bd-l8x9.5.1`.

## Tests / Validation
- E2E smoke coverage: starting the VFX screen must not panic (bd-l8x9.8.1).
- Adapter-specific tests live under `bd-l8x9.6.*`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hi-res metaballs/plasma match or exceed current demo fidelity.
- No duplicated core algorithms inside the demo.
- No per-frame allocations in steady state.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:21.939759537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:29:05.406333508Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:21.939759537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:21.939759537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:21.939759537Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":205,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Notes

This is the fidelity step: once `bd-l8x9.6` exists, the demo can regain sub-cell resolution effects while still using the library code.

Implementation outline:
- Replace the Phase 2 cell-space backdrop rendering with:
  - `MetaballsFx::render_to_painter(...)` (via adapter)
  - `PlasmaFx::render_to_painter(...)` (via adapter)
  - `Canvas::from_painter(&painter)` to render
- Keep Painter reuse from Phase 1.

Acceptance:
- [ ] metaballs/plasma match (or exceed) current wow factor
- [ ] no duplicated core algorithms inside demo
- [ ] no per-frame allocations","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:58:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-l8x9.5.4","title":"Demo UX: Fix CLI Help Screen List (Include VFX + Correct Numbering)","description":"## Task
Update the CLI help screen list to include the Visual Effects screen and correct numbering/order.

## Scope
- Ensure ScreenId ordering matches help output.
- Include VFX screen label and numeric shortcut.

## Tests / Validation
- Snapshot test for help screen output.
- Unit test: screen list length matches ScreenId::ALL.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Help screen shows all screens with correct indices.
- No mismatch between numeric shortcuts and actual screen IDs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:05:37.246272720Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:14:43.963242071Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","docs","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8x9.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:05:37.246272720Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":215,"issue_id":"bd-l8x9.5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Context

`crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/cli.rs` has a hardcoded screen list that currently diverges from `ScreenId::ALL` (see `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs`).

This causes:
- user confusion (`--screen=N` help doesn’t match actual screens)
- E2E scripts that appear to contradict help text

## Change

- Update the HELP_TEXT screen table to match the real order + include VisualEffects as screen 12.
- Consider updating the keybinding docs too:
  - number keys cover only 1..10 (`1..9` and `0`), while Tab cycles all.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `--help` screen list matches real screen order
- [ ] VisualEffects is documented as screen 12
- [ ] Keybinding docs reflect the 12-screen reality","created_at":"2026-02-02T23:05:43Z"}]}
